text: I love you more than coffee, but please don't make me prove it.
author: Unknown

text: You're the reason I look down at my phone and smile. Then walk into a pole.
author: Unknown

text: I love you with all my butt. I would say heart, but my butt is bigger.
author: Unknown

text: You make me smile so much my face hurts — and I still can't stop.
author: Unknown

text: You're my favourite notification.
author: Unknown

text: I'd pick you even if I were the last person on Earth and there was no one else. That's how much I like you.
author: Unknown

text: You're the cheese to my macaroni.
author: Unknown

text: I love you even when I'm hungry — and that's saying something.
author: Unknown

text: If you were a vegetable, you'd be a cute-cumber.
author: Unknown

text: Being deeply loved by someone gives you strength, but loving you gives me laughter.
author: Unknown

text: You make my heart do that stupid little flip thing.
author: Unknown

text: You're my favourite person to do absolutely nothing with.
author: Unknown

text: I love you more than pizza — and I'm being completely serious.
author: Unknown

text: You had me at 'we can stay home'.
author: Unknown

text: Loving you is the easiest thing I do all day.
author: Unknown

text: I'd choose you over Wi-Fi. That's true love.
author: Unknown

text: You're my favourite weirdo.
author: Unknown

text: Every love story is beautiful, but ours is my favourite comedy.
author: Unknown

text: I love you more than yesterday. Yesterday you really got on my nerves.
author: Unknown

text: You're the person I want to annoy for the rest of my life.
author: Unknown

text: You laugh at my terrible jokes like they're the best thing you've heard all day, and I catch myself doing the same with yours.
author: Unknown

text: Watching you dance in the kitchen while burning dinner is my favorite kind of chaos.
author: Unknown

text: You text me the most mundane updates, and I realize these are the moments that anchor me.
author: Unknown

text: You leave the porch light on even when you know I'll be late.
author: Unknown

text: You keep my favorite mug on the counter even when it's my turn to wash it.
author: Unknown

text: You fold my sweater the way I never manage to, and I pretend not to notice.
author: Unknown

text: You always leave your socks in odd places, and I collect them like small proofs of our shared space.
author: Unknown

text: You mock my choice of tea, then drink half of it anyway.
author: Unknown

text: We dance in the living room to songs neither of us can name correctly.
author: Unknown

text: You steal my socks and wear them around the house, claiming they're warmer that way.
author: Unknown

text: I catch you smiling at my terrible jokes and wonder what else you'll pretend to enjoy for my sake.
author: Unknown

text: You always save the corner piece of the cake, knowing it's the one I pretend not to want.
author: Unknown

text: You always steal the last bite of dessert, and I let you because watching your smile is sweeter.
author: Unknown

text: You remember how I take my tea even on days when I forget myself.
author: Unknown

text: Your terrible singing in the car makes me laugh harder than any song on the radio.
author: Unknown

text: You always leave the last sip of wine for me, pretending it's because you're full.
author: Unknown

text: Watching you dance in the kitchen while burning toast is my favorite kind of morning.
author: Unknown

text: You text me pictures of clouds that look like my bad hair days.
author: Unknown

text: You steal my socks on purpose and then deny it with that small smile I can't resist calling out.
author: Unknown

text: You always let me pick the movie first, then pretend my choice was yours all along.
author: Unknown

text: You hum off-key while washing dishes, turning chores into something I linger for.
author: Unknown

text: You always steal the last bite of dessert, and I let you because your grin makes the sacrifice worth it.
author: Unknown

text: You laugh at my terrible jokes like they're clever, and suddenly the room feels lighter.
author: Unknown

text: Your socks never match, yet I find myself matching them in the laundry just to see your surprised smile.
author: Unknown
